parsed_review.summary: CP00-230 is a descriptor-only RP06 closeout pack (risk C, 150 units, RP06.P08.M06.S09-RP06.P09.M03.S10) covering P08 Hermes-receipt micros and P09 review-gate micros. Reviewed read-only via Read/Grep/Glob. The pack binding (registry.js:566-581) matches the baseline exactly and chains correctly from CP229 (next_subphase S09 = first_unit) to CP231 (next_subphase S11 = last_unit S10 + 1). Deliverable and micro-title distributions in DMS_CORE_CP230_REQUIREMENTS (registry.js:3651-3733) match the prompt baseline (hermes_evidence 42, claude_review 14, implementation 73, test 9, security_audit 8, ui 4 = 150; P08=100/P09=50). All 7 required_public_exports exist (2 in service.js, 5 in validators.js) and are wired through index.js. The authority boundary is enforced in code and validated: claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, go_no_go_verdict_format.claude_final_approval_claimed=false, claude_review_packet.read_only=true (validators.js:7241-7253; asserted in model.test.js:2762 and validate-rp06-dms-core-contract.mjs:1849-1850). No-write/no-real-data boundaries are fully attested (service.js freezeCp230Result 5407-5451, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false) and the Hermes packet is descriptor-only (receipt_shape descriptor_only...no_write), confirming Hermes is control-plane review-packet/receipt only, not product runtime. The pack-level production_ready claim is correctly WITHHELD: manifest.json and command-evidence.json both report production_ready=false with status implemented_pending_review / pending_final_closeout, deferring enterprise trust to required human approval — gates preserved, not weakened. Review-hardening rejection criteria (auth_failure, stdout_0_bytes, malformed_json, fenced_json_plus_prose, tool_call_shaped_output, claude/enterprise-trust claims) are present (registry.js:1390-1399); readiness auth preflight passed with valid non-empty JSON. Tests and the validate script source the plan pack and contract from live JSON files (not self-referential fixtures), and validators push genuine drift errors; no stubs/TODOs in source. No findings at any severity. PASS issued as an independent read-only reviewer; this is not final approval and does not by itself close the enterprise trust claim or the standing goal.
